typical_question_structure: Passage 1 (Discursive/Factual): 12 Marks. Mix of 1-mark (MCQ/Objective) and 2-mark (Short Answer) questions.
Passage 2 (Case-Based): 10 Marks. Data interpretation, MCQs, and Analytical Short Answers.
board_pattern_intelligence.average_mark_distribution: Questions are split into 1-mark (approx 6-8 questions) and 2-mark (approx 2-3 questions) per passage. No long answers.
board_pattern_intelligence.difficulty_trend_last_years: Shift from direct 'find-the-line' questions to 'interpret-the-phrase' and 'textual evidence' questions. MCQs have close distractors.

15_minute_execution_algorithm: Step 1 (0:00-0:30): Read the Questions FIRST. Do not read the passage yet. Scan questions 1-5 to identify keywords (dates, names, capitalized words).
Step 2 (0:30-5:30): Skim the Passage. Read the first and last sentence of every paragraph carefully. Read the rest at high speed, underlining keywords found in Step 1.
Step 3 (5:30-10:00): Attack Direct Questions. Answer the 'What', 'When', 'Where' questions immediately. These answers are usually sequential in the text.

Step 4 (10:00-12:30): Tackle Inference/2-Mark Questions. Locate the relevant paragraph. Read 2 lines before and 2 lines after the keyword. Formulate answer using the 'Statement + Because + Text reference' structure.
Step 5 (12:30-14:00): Vocabulary & MCQs. Use elimination. For vocabulary, plug the options back into the sentence to see which fits contextually.
Step 6 (14:00-15:00): Final Sanity Check. Ensure 2-mark answers have 2 distinct points. Check if 'Textual Evidence' questions actually contain a quote.

question_type_attack_models.recognition_signal: Words like 'Give evidence', 'Quote the line', 'Prove that...'
question_type_attack_models.answer_construction_rule: Copy the exact phrase or sentence from the passage inside quotation marks.
question_type_attack_models.word_limit_rule: Do not explain unless asked. Just the quote is often sufficient or a brief intro + quote.
question_type_attack_models.common_trap: Paraphrasing instead of quoting. You lose marks if you rewrite the evidence.

question_type_attack_models.recognition_signal: Words like 'Why', 'Infer', 'What does the author imply', 'Comment on...'
question_type_attack_models.answer_construction_rule: Structure: [Direct Answer/Thesis] + [Because/As indicated by] + [Reference to Text].
question_type_attack_models.minimal_interpretation_rule: Do not use outside knowledge. Answer must be supported strictly by the passage.
question_type_attack_models.common_trap: Writing too much generic philosophy. Stick to the specific context of the paragraph.

question_type_attack_models.recognition_signal: Words like 'Replace the underlined word', 'Find a word meaning...', 'Analogy (X : Y :: A : ?)'
question_type_attack_models.context_scanning_rule: Locate the target sentence. Read it blanking out the difficult word. Predict a simple synonym. Match prediction with options.
question_type_attack_models.elimination_strategy: Eliminate options that are the wrong part of speech (e.g., if looking for a noun, cross out verbs).

question_type_attack_models.answer_construction_rule: Compare data points (Highest vs Lowest). Look for trends (Increasing/Decreasing). Cite specific numbers/percentages in the answer.
question_type_attack_models.word_limit_rule: Keep it factual. 'The data shows a 20% increase...' is better than 'It went up a lot...'
question_type_attack_models.common_trap: Ignoring the units (e.g., writing '50' instead of '50 million' or '50%').

question_type_attack_models.verification_process: Find the exact sentence in the text. If the text contradicts the statement 100%, it is False. If it supports it 100%, it is True.
question_type_attack_models.common_trap: Writing True/False without the justification when the question asks for it.

word_limit_discipline_system.1_mark_answer_range: 10-15 words. One complete sentence or a direct phrase.
word_limit_discipline_system.2_mark_answer_range: 30-40 words. Two distinct points or one developed point with evidence.
word_limit_discipline_system.why_excess_writing_loses_marks: Examiners look for keywords. Fluff hides keywords. Extra writing increases risk of grammar errors.
word_limit_discipline_system.tight_answer_example_model: Q: Why was the author stressed? A: The author was stressed due to the impending deadline and lack of resources (Para 3).

panic_control_protocol: If a word is unknown: Ignore it. Read the sentence without it. Guess the meaning from the 'vibe' (positive/negative) of the surrounding text.
If an answer is not found: Mark the question and move on. The answer often reveals itself while looking for the next one.
If time is running out: Switch to 'Scan and Hunt' mode. Stop reading sentences; look only for the specific nouns in the question.
accuracy_improvement_checklist: Did I answer the specific question asked? (e.g., 'How' vs 'Why')
Did I include units in data questions?
Did I close quotation marks for textual evidence?
Did I check if the question asks for 'One' reason or 'Two' reasons?
common_mark_losing_behaviours: Copying entire paragraphs for a 1-mark answer.
Giving personal opinions instead of passage-based facts.
Missing the 'NOT' in questions like 'Which of these is NOT true?'
Spending 10 minutes on a single difficult vocabulary question.
